@@ -549,13 +549,13 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","title":"Expand crash, concurrency, and filesystem realism coverage","description":"## Why
The current concurrency coverage proves some lock semantics, but it does not yet model real agent swarms, crash timing, filesystem failures, or prolonged mixed usage on one workspace.

## Scope
Expand destructive realism: crash injection, multi-process contention, filesystem anomalies, and long-running stress.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reliability tests model the kinds of failures users actually trigger accidentally.
- We can answer "what happens if the process dies here?" for the critical write/sync phases.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:34.087986025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:50.935545188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chaos","concurrency","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:34.087986025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:50.934904899Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":196,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track intent: move from 'SQLite locking works under a narrow benchmark' to 'the workspace survives hostile real-world timing and storage conditions.'","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","title":"Add a crash-injection matrix for all critical mutation and sync phases","description":"## Purpose
Add crash-injection coverage across the critical mutation and sync phases.

## Required Operations
- create
- update
- close/reopen/defer/undefer
- delete
- label/comment mutation
- sync import/export/rebuild/recovery paths

## Required Crash Points
- before primary write
- after primary write but before derived-state rebuild
- during temp-file write
- before/after rename
- before dirty-flag clear
- during/after backup/quarantine creation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each crash point has explicit expected post-restart behavior.
- No crash leaves the workspace silently misclassified as healthy.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35.916711828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:54:51.704515987Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:54:51.704253666Z","close_reason":"Added crash-injection matrix covering mutation and sync recovery boundaries","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash-consistency","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:49.444077784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:58.411766251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35.916711828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":197,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note: this bead is about interruption semantics, not throughput. Prefer fewer operations with well-defined crash points over broad but shallow coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","title":"Broaden multi-process and mixed-agent contention scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Expand concurrency coverage from narrow lock-timeout scenarios to realistic multi-process and mixed-agent usage.

## Cover
- concurrent writers on one workspace
- read/write races across commands
- multiple agents issuing different command families interleaved
- routed/external workspace access during local mutation
- background processes touching the same `.beads` directory

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests define which outcomes are acceptable vs unacceptable.
- Contention results in bounded failure/retry behavior, not drift or corruption.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:38:47.309252200Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T11:31:29.228335520Z","close_reason":"Expanded bounded-failure concurrency coverage and validated integrity invariants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agents","concurrency","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:09.543615058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":198,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Important consideration: success here does not mean every race magically succeeds. It means failures are explicit, bounded, and non-corrupting.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39Z"},{"id":218,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Working this now in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs. Scope: add coverage for interleaved command families on one workspace, plus external BEADS_DIR/status access against the same workspace during local mutation, with final integrity assertions to distinguish acceptable lock contention from corruption/drift.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:07:24Z"},{"id":219,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs. Added env-clearing/env-aware helper coverage, broadened contention assertions to bounded explicit failures vs corruption, added interleaved-command-family and external-BEADS_DIR/background-status scenarios, and tightened existing concurrency cases to assert final integrity based on successful operations. Verification: rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:31:44Z"},{"id":221,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added broader contention coverage in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs for mixed command families, routed/external access, background sync status checks, and actor-oriented mutation scenarios. Also stabilized e2e_concurrent_writes_succeed_with_retry with an explicit lock timeout for deterministic remote verification. Verified with rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency e2e_concurrent_writes_succeed_with_retry -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, and cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:38:47Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","title":"Model filesystem realism and external interference scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Model filesystem and storage anomalies that hermetic tempdir tests almost never hit.

## Cover
- sidecar mismatch and disappearance
- rename failure / interrupted atomic export
- disk-full or temp-file allocation failure
- permissions changes
- external sync/editor/cloud-drive interference on `.beads`
- unusual HOME/path/env interactions that affect discovery and storage location

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests assert data preservation and honest diagnostics.
- We understand which anomalies are tolerated, which trigger repair, and which require operator intervention.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39.716569260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:26:01.899488434Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["filesystem","ops","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:19.121325297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39.716569260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This is where lab conditions diverge sharply from the field. If a scenario only happens on a user's real filesystem, the current tempdir-first suite is likely blind to it.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41Z"},{"id":228,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Workspace-path audit progress: fixed  redirect provenance, fixed  so missing explicit metadata JSONL no longer falls back to discovered legacy JSONL, fixed  so configured external JSONL paths are validated with the external-path policy instead of being flagged as outside .beads, and strengthened workspace replay assertions to use resolved database/custom metadata paths. Verification is currently limited to rustfmt/git-diff checks and targeted reasoning because the rch worker fleet is unreachable right now and I am avoiding local cargo fallback per repo rules.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:37:53Z"},{"id":229,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Correction to previous note: fixed where redirect provenance; fixed doctor so a missing explicit metadata JSONL path no longer falls back to a discovered legacy JSONL; fixed doctor so a configured external JSONL path is validated with the external-path policy instead of being flagged as outside .beads; and strengthened workspace replay assertions to use the resolved database/custom metadata paths. Verification is currently limited to rustfmt/git-diff checks and targeted reasoning because the rch worker fleet is unreachable right now and I am avoiding local cargo fallback per repo rules.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:38:01Z"},{"id":230,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"IcyCitadel","text":"Progress update: audited workspace discovery, where/info/doctor path reporting, and workspace failure replay coverage. Fixed redirect provenance in `where`, fixed `doctor` so a missing explicitly configured metadata JSONL path is surfaced instead of silently falling back to a discovered legacy file, corrected the config unit test for external DB override workspace fallback and added the missing no-workspace error case, and updated workspace replay assertions to validate the resolved database path from `where --json` for custom metadata DB paths. Verification: rustfmt --check passed on touched files; git diff --check passed; rch remote tests passed for discover_beads_dir_with_cli_from_* filters, resolve_where_output_preserves_redirect_origin, and test_collect_doctor_report_surfaces_missing_explicit_metadata_jsonl.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:47Z"},{"id":231,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"IcyCitadel","text":"Follow-up: full rch cargo check --all-targets did not expose new code errors in these touched paths, but it failed remotely on vmi1227854 with No space left on device while compiling unrelated integration-test targets. A narrower rch cargo check --lib still surfaced one local warning in doctor.rs (unused std::fs::self import); I removed that import locally afterward.","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:03:34Z"}]}

- We understand which anomalies are tolerated, which trigger repair, and which require operator intervention.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39.716569260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:10:30.329596831Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["filesystem","ops","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:19.121325297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39.716569260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This is where lab conditions diverge sharply from the field. Verification is currently limited to rustfmt/git-diff checks and targeted reasoning because the rch worker fleet is unreachable right now and I am avoiding local cargo fallback per repo rules.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:37:53Z"},{"id":229,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Correction to previous note: fixed where redirect provenance; fixed doctor so a missing explicit metadata JSONL path no longer falls back to a discovered legacy JSONL; fixed doctor so a configured external JSONL path is validated with the external-path policy instead of being flagged as outside .beads; and strengthened workspace replay assertions to use the resolved database/custom metadata paths. Verification is currently limited to rustfmt/git-diff checks and targeted reasoning because the rch worker fleet is unreachable right now and I am avoiding local cargo fallback per repo rules.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:38:01Z"},{"id":230,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"IcyCitadel","text":"Progress update: audited workspace discovery, where/info/doctor path reporting, and workspace failure replay coverage. Fixed redirect provenance in `where`, fixed `doctor` so a missing explicitly configured metadata JSONL path is surfaced instead of silently falling back to a discovered legacy file, corrected the config unit test for external DB override workspace fallback and added the missing no-workspace error case, and updated workspace replay assertions to validate the resolved database path from `where --json` for custom metadata DB paths. Verification: rustfmt --check passed on touched files; git diff --check passed; rch remote tests passed for discover_beads_dir_with_cli_from_* filters, resolve_where_output_preserves_redirect_origin, and test_collect_doctor_report_surfaces_missing_explicit_metadata_jsonl.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:47Z"},{"id":231,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"IcyCitadel","text":"Follow-up: full rch cargo check --all-targets did not expose new code errors in these touched paths, but it failed remotely on vmi1227854 with No space left on device while compiling unrelated integration-test targets. A narrower rch cargo check --lib still surfaced one local warning in doctor.rs (unused std::fs::self import); I removed that import locally afterward.","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:03:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","title":"Add a long-running single-workspace stress suite","description":"## Purpose
Create a long-running stress suite that reuses one workspace across many commands and processes to surface drift, stale assumptions, and accumulated-state bugs.

## Cover
- repeated issue lifecycle operations
- repeated sync/import/export status checks
- intermittent failures and restarts
- mixed human-style and agent-style command ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- The suite can run for a meaningful duration without manual babysitting.
- Failures leave behind enough artifacts to classify and reproduce.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:35:25.309497352Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:35:25.309127179Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic long-lived single-workspace stress suite with mixed create/update/label/comment/close/reopen/sync/read/expected-failure flow; verified targeted stress test, workspace evolution tests, fmt, check, clippy, and ubs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["long-lived","reliability","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:28.600633433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:37.659700575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":200,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists because the product is a stateful CLI, not a stateless request handler. Bugs that require dozens or hundreds of operations on the same workspace will never show up in per-test fresh-init patterns.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","title":"Harden detection, recovery, and no-data-loss guarantees","description":"## Why
Even with better tests, the product must behave safely when it encounters a damaged or inconsistent workspace in production. Detection, recovery, and operator messaging need to be consistent and trustworthy.

## Scope
Unify anomaly classification, preserve evidence before repair, verify post-recovery state, and make `doctor`/startup/write behavior agree.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Automatic recovery is explicit, reversible, and validated.
- We do not report "OK" for workspaces that are only partially readable or that fail on writes.
- Recovery logic never silently hides potential data loss.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43.783459850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:09.162394901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","recovery","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43.783459850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:59.944386891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:09.161785269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":201,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track is where field pain becomes product behavior. The product must either operate safely, recover safely, or stop with actionable evidence; the current gray zone in between is unacceptable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","title":"Unify anomalous workspace classification across startup, doctor, and write paths","description":"## Purpose
Create one canonical anomaly classifier that every entry point uses when evaluating a workspace: startup/open, explicit `doctor`, mutation paths, sync, and recovery logic.

## Scope
- define shared anomaly classes for malformed SQLite pages, WAL/SHM mismatch, JSONL parse drift, metadata contradictions, stale dirty/export state, and broken derived caches
- remove ad hoc command-specific interpretations of the same evidence
- ensure structured errors and operator-facing diagnostics use the same language

## Acceptance Criteria
- A given workspace snapshot receives the same classification regardless of which command hits it first.
- Recovery and write decisions are driven by named health states rather than scattered heuristics.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:49:21.619173305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:51:17.532667668Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:51:17.532378717Z","close_reason":"Created src/health.rs with canonical WorkspaceHealth/AnomalyClass/WorkspaceClassification types. Integrated into OpenStorageResult::classify() and doctor JSON output. 7 unit tests + replay harness tests pass. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["classification","diagnostics","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:47.622950600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:57.607408234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:49:21.619173305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead is the hinge point for the whole hardening track. If startup says “healthy enough” while writes or sync say “catastrophic corruption,” the user experiences chaos even when each individual code path looks locally reasonable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","title":"Quarantine corrupt DB artifacts before automatic rebuild or repair","description":"## Purpose
When corruption or unsafe drift is detected, preserve the original DB artifacts before any rebuild, retry, or repair action mutates evidence.

## Scope
- move or snapshot suspect SQLite artifacts and sidecars into a clearly labeled quarantine area
- record why quarantine happened and what command triggered it
- make rebuild-from-JSONL and similar repair flows operate on fresh targets rather than on top of ambiguous debris

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery never silently overwrites the only copy of a damaged DB or WAL.
- Operators can inspect and attach quarantined artifacts to incident reports.
- Automatic repair paths become auditable and reversible.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:50:44.050503419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:57:49.905156647Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:57:49.904618319Z","close_reason":"Truncated WAL/SHM sidecars are quarantined into .br_recovery before startup open instead of being deleted; added regression coverage for evidence preservation and valid-WAL no-op behavior.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["quarantine","recovery","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:50:44.050503419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:06.949746141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The root reliability principle here is evidence preservation. A “successful” automatic rebuild that destroys the original failing artifacts is still a product failure because it erases the operator’s ability to diagnose, prove, or safely roll back what happened.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:24Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","title":"Verify post-recovery invariants and forbid silent state loss","description":"## Purpose
Every recovery path must end with explicit postconditions proving that user-visible state and internal invariants are acceptable before normal writes continue.

## Scope
- verify issue graph, comments, labels, events, metadata, export markers, sidecars, and derived caches after repair
- detect silent data loss, ID collisions, truncated exports, stale `last_touched`, or self-contradictory counters
- refuse to declare success if postconditions fail

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery paths have a machine-checkable “safe to proceed” verdict.
- Silent partial repair is treated as failure, not success.
- Tactical corruption fixes roll into a shared invariant-validation framework.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:08.702874952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:28.029445713Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["invariants","recovery","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:08.702874952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:16.123909606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:25.768255051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-v13g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:36.942963087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":205,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"A retry-after-rebuild path is only defensible if the product can prove the rebuilt workspace is coherent. This bead turns “it seems to work now” into explicit post-recovery evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:28Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","title":"Expand doctor to detect drift, sidecar anomalies, and partial-functionality traps","description":"## Purpose
Make `br doctor` the authoritative workspace inspection tool for drift and partial-breakage states, not just an optimistic health check.

## Scope
- detect DB/JSONL divergence, invalid or contradictory metadata, suspicious sidecar leftovers, and partially functioning workspaces where reads pass but writes fail
- expose actionable structured diagnostics and recommended next steps
- ensure doctor classification aligns with startup and mutation decisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- `doctor` can flag the classes of failures that users are actually hitting in the field.
- A workspace that is unsafe for writes is never reported as simply “OK.”","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:51.118030931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:03:37.907454310Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","doctor","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:35.332546461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:51.118030931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:44.594245547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The immediate user complaint is exactly this gap: the system can look healthy from one angle and then fail catastrophically on the next write. `doctor` has to become the honest reporter of those partial-functionality traps.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:31Z"},{"id":232,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress note: patched `br doctor` to add explicit `db.sidecars` error diagnostics and `db.recovery_artifacts` warnings, plus local sidecar quarantine-in-recovery behavior for `doctor --repair`. Added targeted unit tests and a new E2E case for anomalous WAL-without-SHM repair. Claim is still graph-blocked by beads_rust-rsei.4.1 / beads_rust-rsei.4, so this is progress without status transition until verification finishes.","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:29:52Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","title":"Verify post-recovery invariants and forbid silent state loss","description":"## Purpose
Every recovery path must end with explicit postconditions proving that user-visible state and internal invariants are acceptable before normal writes continue.

## Scope
- verify issue graph, comments, labels, events, metadata, export markers, sidecars, and derived caches after repair
- detect silent data loss, ID collisions, truncated exports, stale `last_touched`, or self-contradictory counters
- refuse to declare success if postconditions fail

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery paths have a machine-checkable “safe to proceed” verdict.
- Silent partial repair is treated as failure, not success.
- Tactical corruption fixes roll into a shared invariant-validation framework.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:08.702874952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:16:24.686225707Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:16:24.685955310Z","close_reason":"Added post-rebuild validation for JSONL recovery: issue counts and orphaned issue references are verified before recovery succeeds","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["invariants","recovery","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:08.702874952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:16.123909606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:25.768255051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-v13g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:36.942963087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":205,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"A retry-after-rebuild path is only defensible if the product can prove the rebuilt workspace is coherent. This bead turns “it seems to work now” into explicit post-recovery evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:28Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","title":"Expand doctor to detect drift, sidecar anomalies, and partial-functionality traps","description":"## Purpose
Make `br doctor` the authoritative workspace inspection tool for drift and partial-breakage states, not just an optimistic health check.

## Scope
- detect DB/JSONL divergence, invalid or contradictory metadata, suspicious sidecar leftovers, and partially functioning workspaces where reads pass but writes fail
- expose actionable structured diagnostics and recommended next steps
- ensure doctor classification aligns with startup and mutation decisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- `doctor` can flag the classes of failures that users are actually hitting in the field.
- A workspace that is unsafe for writes is never reported as simply “OK.”","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:51.118030931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:15:35.230897125Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:15:35.230587315Z","close_reason":"Doctor workspace health now incorporates drift, sidecar, JSONL, recovery artifact, null-default, and rollback-only write-probe diagnostics instead of reporting only file-state health.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","doctor","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:35.332546461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:51.118030931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:44.594245547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The immediate user complaint is exactly this gap: the system can look healthy from one angle and then fail catastrophically on the next write. `doctor` has to become the honest reporter of those partial-functionality traps.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:31Z"},{"id":232,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress note: patched `br doctor` to add explicit `db.sidecars` error diagnostics and `db.recovery_artifacts` warnings, plus local sidecar quarantine-in-recovery behavior for `doctor --repair`. Added targeted unit tests and a new E2E case for anomalous WAL-without-SHM repair. Claim is still graph-blocked by beads_rust-rsei.4.1 / beads_rust-rsei.4, so this is progress without status transition until verification finishes.","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:29:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","title":"Fix spurious primary-key errors after successful JSONL-only writes","description":"## Problem
During this planning session, `br create --no-db`, `br comments add --no-db`, and `br dep add --no-db` successfully persisted changes to JSONL and then still returned `DATABASE_ERROR` with `PRIMARY KEY constraint failed`. That makes automation believe the write failed even though state changed underneath it.

## Repro Shape
- create or update beads in `--no-db` mode against the current long-lived workspace
- observe the issue/comment/dependency present afterward
- observe the command exit non-zero with a bogus primary-key error after the successful write

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSONL-only write commands return success when the write succeeded
- duplicate-write protection still works when a write genuinely conflicts
- tests cover issue creation, comment creation, and dependency creation in `--no-db` mode","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:00:57.722543395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:51:38.339885652Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:51:38.326075963Z","close_reason":"Reduced export_hashes rewrite batching to avoid false PRIMARY KEY failures after successful --no-db JSONL writes, and added storage/e2e regressions that cover create, comments add, and dep add.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["jsonl-only","reliability","write-path"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:00:57.722543395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":214,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead was discovered while creating the reliability program itself, which is exactly why it belongs here: the planning/tooling path should not be a special case that silently normalizes bad write semantics.","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:01:01Z"},{"id":217,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Root caused the false --no-db PRIMARY KEY failure to the export_hashes rewrite path, reduced the rewrite batch size, and added storage + e2e regressions for create/comments/dep add.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:51:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","title":"Install release gates, observability, and operator guidance for reliability","description":"## Why
Reliability work is not complete when the code seems better locally. It is complete when regressions are hard to ship, anomalies are visible, and operators have a playbook for field failures.

## Scope
Turn the new invariants, fixture corpus, realism suites, and recovery paths into mandatory release discipline and operator guidance.

## Deliverables
- CI/release gates for real-world reliability suites
- structured observability around anomaly classification and recovery
- updated disaster-recovery and troubleshooting docs
- a non-hermetic smoke profile that exercises ambient-env and existing-workspace behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- reliability regressions become release blockers rather than post-release surprises
- operators can diagnose and escalate failures without reverse-engineering the code","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:54.326583926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:34.054171443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ops","release-gating","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:54.326583926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:18.751657304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:28.736789160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:37.692812501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":202,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists to turn architectural hardening into release discipline; otherwise the new realism work will remain optional and decay over time.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:57Z"},{"id":207,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track exists to prevent a repeat of the current situation where the suite is green but the field experience is red. The outcome has to be operational, not just architectural.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","title":"Make real-world reliability suites mandatory in CI and release verification","description":"## Purpose
Promote the real-world reliability suites from optional or ad hoc checks into mandatory gates for CI, release verification, and high-risk changes.

## Cover
- failure-corpus replay
- crash-injection suites
- long-lived-workspace stress
- doctor/recovery postcondition checks

## Acceptance Criteria
- shipping without these suites requires an explicit conscious override, not omission by default
- release verification documents the outcome of the reliability gates","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:57.611605676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:37.124139035Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release-gating","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:53.826363038Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:03.126099578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:12.811292518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:22.300269735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:31.628692974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:57.611605676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":208,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"If the new realism suites do not gate merges and releases, they will slowly rot into optional diagnostics and we will be back where we started.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:37Z"}]}
